Power: Produces liquid darkness that could be used to teleport over a wide range.
Comments: I know that there are technically multiple Abhorrors, this was just the one whose powers felt sufficiently different to give it its own ranking. It doesn't seem to have any Trump effects, but at base, this clone's power is probably even better than the original Grue's.

Power: Intelligence scales with the size, scope, and complexity of the problems presented to him.
Comments: His ability to engineer traps gives him a small boost to AP, defense, and stealth but it really doesn't work if he doesn't have the home-field advantage.

Power: Could turn into, sling blasts of acid, and move as a tidal wave of strong acid.
Comments: I hate this guy but he's a powerhouse. Going face-to-face with Zion and surviving (I think?) is a feat that just automatically makes him a strong cape.

Power: Metallokinetic that can crudely move and shape metal, wield heavy metal armor and weapons without encumberment, and slowly strengthen, refine, and repair metals.
Comments: I'd completely forgotten about this guy until I got like 150 capes into this spreadsheet. I'd say he's just a little worse off in the metallokinesis department overall than Cuff, but not by a lot. They're good at different things, and as far as brutes go, this guy is solid and adaptable.

Power: Possesses redundant biology that operates at peak capacity so long as alive, flight, and the ability to use body with no fear of harm to self.
Comments: I love this guy and wish there was more of him in general.

Power: Creates organic structures that grow exponentially before hardening to a stone-like substance.
Comments: One of the cooler non-really-great-for-combat powers.

Power: Restored to perfect condition every 4.3 sec.
Comments: Discount grayboy, defensively.

Power: Invincibility via body in stasis, flight, and vastly enhanced strength. Also has perfect eidetic recall, enhanced processing/learning, and consequent mastery of many skills.
Comments: The OG flying brick, +2 to manipulation because if she tells me to do something I'm immediately folding (or because of her cold reading and knack for exploiting emotions I guess).

Power: Can create mid-air portals from which to launch volleys of weapons.
Comments: Racist discount Gilgamesh. Currently crying in hell after watching his empire get cooked by a black man and a big fish.

Power: Specialized in computers and AI.
Comments: Honestly, how do I even rate this guy? I was scoring Tinkers based on their work but his work is an entirely different character/is the toolset of another different character.

Power: Limited time transformation to beast form with power nullification scream.
Comments: Probably the least interesting of the Teeth members ngl.

Power: Can meld with inorganic material and reshape it.
Comments: Wildbow did my boy raw as hell killing him off-screen like that.

Power: Creates black spheres that can pull in opponents.
Comments: A bit speculative but you work with what you have, even if it's just one scene.

Power: Danger sense, can determine threat level of those surrounding an individual based on color. Can also minor produce forcefields and sonic beams.
Comments: That scene where she learned Portuguese was pretty cool, too bad she's with my least favorite team.

Power: Tinker with specializations in miniaturization, hybridization, and efficiency.
Comments: He is one of my favorite characters and one of the best Tinkers in the story due to his sheer versatility in combination with his invention of the nano-thorn. Method tinkers might be OP.

Power: Shapeshifting beast form made of explosions generated through constant matter-to-energy and energy-to-matter conversion.
Comments: This guy is such a cool piece of worldbuilding, he is probably the most destructive non-all-or-nothing cape on the list.

Power: Manipulates kinetic energy.
Comments: For some reason I imagined him and his suit being like the Rhino from the end of the Amazing Spider-Man 2. Anyway, I don't like him but his power is way better than I initially picked up from reading. It's also vague as hell.

Power: Stops people from taking any action that could directly harm him.
Comments: This was a pretty cool fight to see since it locked Taylor out of using her go-to special move (killing a child).

Power: Bomb tinker. Also has an innate understanding of mechanisms and catalysts.
Comments: Probably straight up the best Tinker in terms of attack potency, temporal locking bombs are crazy. The versatility of her attacks is also pretty insane, but she doesn't have much going for her beyond the raw damage she can do.

Power: Imbues kinetic energy on touched objects.
Comments: Takes notes Assault, we've got an actually likable asshole kinetic energy manipulator with a clearly defined power. Guy was actively holding back most times when he was using his powers but they're simple and pretty strong.

Power: Launches at high speeds, ricochets with resulting explosions on impacts, for multiple impacts per launch. Enhanced balance/agility.
Comments: For some reason, I imagined her as taking the shape of an actual ball with a face on it, like a Target-parking-lot-ball-sized version of Ego the Living Planet.

Power: Converts utterances to an energy that can then be converted into a variety of effects, including concussive force and flame.
Comments: Talor beating him up was such a cool moment for her, but it makes him look a bit weaker than I think he actually is. Inugami syndrome.

Power: Slowly replaces the environment within a fair radius with an otherworldly forest. Cannot move beyond the forest effect, but can extend radius by standing at the perimeter.
Comments: This guy is cool but I see like zero applications of his ability for conflict.

Power: Can create forcefields and deflectors.
Comments: I didn't even remember this guy, which is probably what the PRT wanted given the public relations disaster he is.

Power: Charges herself up with immobility to grant enhanced speed, reflexes, strength, and electromagnetic telekinesis.
Comments: Wildbow giving her an interlude right before she died was so foul. Also needing to stand still to charge up your power is such a big drawback that I don't think even the super speed, strength, and invincibility make up for it.

Power: Glasswork tinker.
Comments: The only example we have of her work is a device that can turn inorganic matter into glass, which is still pretty cool.

Power: Construction drone tinker.
Comments: This guy probably could have been useful post-leviathan and GM.

Power: Can morph dogs into massive monsters. Warped psychology that better interacts with dogs rather than humans.
Comments: Probably the second best-written character behind Taylor herself, and she has a solid, fleshed-out power that's decent at pretty much everything you need in a direct confrontation (except stealth). Wolfspider is real and cannon, Wildbow just ran out of yuri juice at the end of the novel and accidentally wrote about her having sex with a man and not Taylor Hebert.

Power: Distorts the size of individual body parts
Comments: I like this guy despite the fact that his existence opposes my gay Bitch headcannon. Fun power, but it's not that great in a fight.

Power: Teleports, with momentary existence in all spaces between departure and arrival, allowing for katana strikes at intervening foes.
Comments: I try not to be a weeb but she's possibly the rawest background character in the series. Her power is great and she basically does Jack Slash's main power better than he does, with some Oni Lee on the side. It's nothing personal, kid.

Power: Wet tinker, specializes in creating life forms.
Comments: Possibly my favorite tinker specialization, and definitely a stronger one from what little we got to see in the story. Despite the fact that trying to make it was so crazy that even Bonesaw refused to do it, I really want to see what the Morrígan would have been able to do if it was completed.

Power: Telekinetically enhanced strength and durability that scales with the size and reaction of his audience. Also capable of stunning/slowing his enemies.
Comments: I don't like him but he has a pretty cool power. I have no clue how high his strength so I went with what little we got. The 2 in strength is a little counterintuitive given publicity is fuel for his power, but he doesn't need to have it on at all times.

Power: Biology, anatomy, and physiology tinker.
Comments: The most OP (human) tinker, and the main powerhouse behind the S9. Her specialization is so broad and so powerful and her shard likes her so much that she can basically achieve any effect she wants using a variety of means. Also, I thought at the beginning that if Wildbow tried to make any of the S9 sympathetic I'd be mad, and then he did it with arguably the least redeemable one and I loved it.

Power: Makes energy weapons and can turn into a near-invulnerable sphere.
Comments: This is a pretty good straight-up combat power overall, with solid attack and defense with little use outside of that. It sucks that her main defensive option involves confronting her exact phobia though.

Power: Creates small lifeforms that inhabit corpses, grow, and become more dangerous over time/after eating.
Comments: Even though his power is kind of garbage, it provokes some of the strongest "aw hell no I'm not fighting thing" response in the entire series.

Power: Point-blank telekinesis and personal biokinesis for personal healing and self-alteration.
Comments: Cool cape with a very solid powerset. His two powers played off of each other very nicely, though not quite nicely enough for him to survive throwing hands with an Endbringer like he tried to do.

Power: Pyrokinetic and fireproof, can teleport through flames. Psychopathy scales with the amount of fire nearby.
Comments: Probably the saddest S9 member, but her power is pretty insane and its only real weakness besides the mental changes is the lack of reliable defensive options.

Power: Ranged pain inducement, enhanced durability, enhanced strength, cardiovascular system tracking, short-range danger sense, short-range explosive teleport, unrefined matter shaping, short-range mindless rage inducement, pain immunity, perfect accuracy, passes powers and consciousness down to killer.
Comments: Another really cool character by Wildbow, the Teeth as a whole gives such a unique vibe that really adds to the setting. Despite the score I gave her, she has probably one of the best defenses in that not many people even want to kill her, and will go out of their way not to.

Power: Can store/retrieve items and individuals in a pocket dimension.
Comments: I'd forgotten about this guy, but he did pretty well in the fight he was in. I had considered making an "everyday utility" category, and if I had done that, this guy would probably be pretty high up. Honestly, if I had to pick a power to take to the real world to use for normal life, this might be the one.

Power: Super strength and agility augmented with point-blank aerokinesis.
Comments: We didn't get to see him use his aerokinesis, but his raw strength was pretty exceptional. I want to see more South American capes because the whole idea of the villains being more connected to the government than the heroes is really cool.

Power: Giant growth with gravity-altering aura.
Comments: One of the few people on the list who we didn't see in action at all, but his power is easy enough to imagine with the descriptions given. He gets my favor for having the funniest scene in Worm.

Power: Enhanced singing that makes anyone who hears it susceptible to suggestions.
Comments: Her stealth score is kind of weird because while she does have bright yellow feathers and needs to do the obvious act of singing to use her power, her suggestions have solid applications when it comes to subterfuge. Also, seeing a character introduced in an early interlude contribute greatly to the end of Gold Morning was really cool.

Power: Durable healer that accelerates healing by bathing in blood.
Comments: Another one that we didn't really see in the story, except as one of Bonesaw's monster mashups, but the power is simple so scoring isn't too difficult.

Power: Tinker, produced devices, suits, and vehicles relating to movement and teleportation.
Comments: A tinker with a very solid specialization, I wish we saw more of him and what he could do. A wise Skitter once said, "If you can't kill your enemies, bully them into killing themselves." (Note: That last sentence was definitely meant for someone else, but its been months since I wrote it and I can't tell who anymore, so it's staying here).

Power: Can manipulate/sense emotions. Later gained all former Butcher powers.
Comments: Mini-heartbreaker is very strong. Only specific powers or situations can let you resist her control, and if she hadn't gone and signed up with the S9, she probably would have done fairly well as a villain.

Power: Can combine similar items, giving resulting item select qualities of different 'parent' items. Also sees abstract representations of other parahumans' trigger events.
Comments: One of my favorite characters, I love classical heroes. His power was a bit hard to understand, I thought the objects he used stayed separate and he was just picking properties from them. But after I was clear on everything, his power turned out to be one of my favorites as well, since he's like a dimensional material engineering tinker. Also adding Endbringer bits to your gear is raw as hell.

Power: Can control rats.
Comments: If I had to pick one, I'd probably rather control bugs than rats. This seems like a straight downgrade from Taylor's power, though I'm sure that being devoured alive by rats is more terrifying than being stung to death by bugs.

Power: Can create solid mirrors of past events, potentially multiplying attack effectiveness. Also flies.
Comments: Interesting power, but I struggle to think of great applications for it.

Power: Gained durability and ambient momentum dampening effect at will, tied to consequent loss of mobility.
Comments: RIP to a real one.

Power: Super speed in head and legs. Super strength in chest and arms.
Comments: Horrifying power drawback, may he rest in hell.

Power: Generates intensely hot fire from his hands.
Comments: Very simple and direct power, but the fire is hot, so it's pretty alright.

Power: Many weaker powers, including balance, personal pocket dimension, and pyrokinesis if flame is available.
Comments: God's silliest soldier. Probably would have been better off joining an actual circus.

Power: Alters reality in a small area, adjusting physics or natural laws.  Can attune 'frequency' to diminish or disrupt a parahuman's powers, with perfect frequency killing them.
Comments: Discount Stilling is still very strong. Being able to create a perfect defense for most powers, change the laws of physics around you, and kill people beyond their defenses by finding the right frequency is crazy, and only really held back by her range.

Power: Has and can grant complete clairvoyance, also overriding other perception powers
Comments: Does what his name suggests really well, and the potential information overload attack is nice.

Power: Freezes objects in spacetime for a random duration with a touch.
Comments: Dennis, my boy! The ability to shut down any opponent with a touch is fantastic, and the fact that his power can trap people in things like loops of string helps mitigate his only real drawback of needing to touch the target. The panic button for an inviolable defense is also great, but nothing is stopping an enemy from placing a persistently dangerous effect, like covering him in acid and waiting him out.

Power: Causes permanent brain damage to targets in exchange for a temporary boost to her own mental power.
Comments: WTF do you mean you can cause immediate, unblockable brain damage get the hell away from me!

Power: Can split timelines to attempt alternate strategies at will.
Comments: I genuinely cannot wrap my head around the explanation for how his power works. But in any case, his defense rating is as high as it is because no matter what you do to him, he can always set up another timeline where he's been chilling 100 miles away since before the fight began, and not a lot of powers can counter that.

Power: Is shown the "Path to Victory" to anything she wants to do. She knows the steps she needs to take towards a goal, and can perfectly execute those steps.
Comments: I mean, she wins.

Power: Generates pocket dimensions that can stow/produce items and individuals at range, and could form networks to transfer things between them. Only operated ideally when under minimal stress/undistracted.
Comments: Her power is actually pretty okay, assuming she has time to set up a network beforehand.

Power: Keen understanding of movement and fighting styles, could learn and teach the 'perfect style' for a given individual.  Can generate a floating orb that redirects movement in its vicinity.
Comments: Lock her back up she's not built for a Scion fight.

Power: Neurology tinker.
Comments: Strong enough to be partially responsible for the Slaughterhouse 9000.

Power: Powerful regeneration and gains personal adaptations in response to any damage taken.
Comments: With this treasure I summon...

Power: Vastly increased reflexes, personal sonar that could be keyed to disrupt others' senses.
Comments: She's kind of garbage ngl. Plenty of capes could outrange her, outspeed her, or ignore the effects of her powers.

Power: Enhances strength and durability by drinking the blood of others.
Comments: Super strength and durability are something a lot of other capes get for free, but this guy needs several people's worth of blood for a not-all-that-strong power-up.

Power: Generates forcefields within which he can create incinerating heat.
Comments: We didn't see him in action much, so I'm lowballing my estimates for his power, but damn this guy is kind of a badass. He was going up against the S-9000 and survived, and his forcefields were good enough to make him viable as a hero without even using his crazy incineration power.

Power: Can create shades of himself to fight and lift himself. Favorably Manton limited as shades ignore inorganic matter, penetrating armor and passing through walls.
Comments: One of the better E88 members, power-wise, and probably my favorite of them. Wearing armor was a good choice since it kind of rounds out the big weakness of his power that he doesn't have much protecting his main body.

Power: Enhanced strength, durability, and metallokinesis, augmenting agility by manipulating own armor.
Comments: A more solid powerset than I was originally thinking. I really like this take on metallokineses, you almost never see them get based more on agility than whacking, slicing, and restraining people. I also like her a lot as a character, she's a great example of someone who just isn't built for the cape life personality-wise, but pushes through that and accomplishes a lot. That scene where she forces Taylor to apologize to Theo with weaponized niceness is funny as hell too.

Power: Creates a physically enhanced phantom version of himself until his concentration breaks or it takes too much damage.
Comments: Don't think it don't say it don't think it don't say it.

Power: Can produce high recoil 'shotgun' bursts of warped space, disintegration, and gravity, able to shear and destroy virtually anything, as well as fling her back. Can be used as mover power.
Comments: She probably has the most "lopsided" stats, normally a high ranking in one category kind of boosts the others by proxy but her ability is pretty specific. Bonus points in my heart for the great name.

Power: Incrementally empowers items day-by-day, permanently physically enhancing them and giving them unique abilities. Objects are briefly supercharged just after he empowers them.
Comments: Talk about wasted potential, this guy could have been insanely good if he hadn't died so early. Which I guess is the point of his character. Thanks, Wildbow :(

Power: Probabilistic precognition in reflex to receiving questions about future events. Can pick out specific timelines to determine what happens. Has blind spots, but can somewhat get around them with careful questioning.
Comments: The best precog in the series, and probably the child I feel the most bad for in the series. Which is saying a lot.

Power: Creates opaque, inviolable bubbles wherein time is accelerated, making anything that happens inside of them appear to outside observers as if it happened in an instant. Can stagger manifestations to mimic super speed. Also has super strength and durability.
Comments: This guy has one of the cooler powers in the series, and it's pretty damn strong as well. He gets around the only real drawback pretty easily with oxygen tanks, though he couldn't show that off in the Behemoth fight, and he synergizes very well with any cape that needs time to be effective, which notably includes Eidolon. Also, Houston represent!

Power: Pocket dimension tinker.
Comments: The ability to create long-lasting, gated-access, huge-ass pocket dimensions is good.

Power: Can open portals to anywhere in his sensory awareness, including across dimensions. Portals come in all shapes and sizes.
Comments: Judged without connection to any other parahumans, he got the short end of the power stick, but he synergizes so well with so many other powers that it doesn't really matter.

Power: Flight, drops a 'rain' of forcefield pellets that accumulate and hamper those caught in her wake.
Comments: Someone please give her a normal job she doesn't belong out here with the big dawgs.

Power: AI with the ability to replicate other Tinkers' work.
Comments: Wildbow you RAT stop trying to kill my favorite character.

Power: Body absorbs living tissues and develops related body parts, and exhibits endbringer-level strength, and high-level durability and regeneration so long as her core is intact. Vomits stronger, more aggressive clones of any organism she fully consumes, for as long as she can keep them inside of her. Parahumans cannot use their powers when in contact with her, hallucinate their worst memories while inside of her, and parahuman clones have an often stronger variation on the original's powers. Has city-wide parahuman recognition and tracking via a thinker power that interprets the information as a "smell".
Comments: She honestly might've gotten the shortest end of the stick in this superpower lottery. Having the Smurf perfectly engineer the most tragic series of events possible for this character was devious as hell, WiffleBat.

Power: Has a vast store of varied powers. He can draw on a couple at a time, and they take a couple of minutes to reach full potency. He does not control exactly which power he gets in a given situation, as it is dictated by what his shard thinks he needs. He is only able to hold on tightly to a power, or purposely discard it to gamble for a new one.
Comments: As the strongest parahuman went up against the King of Shards, Zion, the Golden Fraud asked him a question. "Are you the strongest because you're Eidolon, or are you Eidolon because you're the strongest." Eidolon smiled and said, "Nah, I'd win." The pair then opened their domains, but Zion didn't know two key things. The first is that in a one-on-one fight, always bet on the High Priest. The second is that he would bear witness to the naked flesh of David, the man who left it all behind, and his overwhelming intensity. As their domains clashed and Eidolon manifested an all-or-nothing shard technique to stop Zion in his tracks, he said "You tried to mess with humanity, but you didn't realize that we alone are the exception. Because throughout the heavens and the Earth, I alone am the honored one." As the Bullied Entity turned to see his dead partner behind him, Eidolon spoke four words, barely audible. "Stand proud, you're strong." Finished but still unsatisfied, Eidolon said, "Looks like I still need worthy opponents. With this treasure I summon... the Endbringers."

Power: Creates blades of air that boomerang back to him.
Comments: You'd better be glad I used up my only allowed lobotomy kaisen on the one directly before this guy alphabetically.

Power: Can move things forward, backward, or pause them in time in ten-second intervals.
Comments: Wildbow please please please expand on who this guy is more, he is the most interesting of the characters who don't get screentime I just need to know how he made Brian hate time travelers please Wildbow I beg you.

Power: Powerful aerokinetic, powers worked optimally only once in a given, lengthy duration (sometimes hours, sometimes days), with limited aeroskinesis otherwise.
Comments: Needs a several-day charge-up to deliver the same force that other parahumans can deliver whenever they want. Possibly the most washed Protectorate member.

Power: Displaces people and objects to and from a dimension filled with water.
Comments: Discount Grey Boy??? Seriously, depending on the activation requirement for his power, this guy could be a real menace. He's not even Manton-limited wtf?

Power: Can destroy inorganic matter with a touch.
Comments: A cool character, but I kept thinking she was going to be more major than she ended up being. Her power is simple and pretty good.

Power: Has limited control over certain types of birds, which he can also give fire resistance and pyrokinetic abilities.
Comments: I unironically love this guy's powers. I mean, they're not good, but they're so funny and creative that I would 100% want him on my team just to watch him fight. #FELIXSWEEP

Power: Grows in size, with a space-warping effect granting increased durability at the same time.
Comments: They share a power, one which is honestly pretty solid, now they get to share a row. Thanks, Inverse-Square Law!

Power: Creates orbs of energy that detonate explosively or concussively, with potentially lethal force and blinding brightness. Can modify the power of orbs, re-absorb them, and is immune to their effects.
Comments: He sure is one of the characters in the story. Not much to say otherwise, but his power is decent.

Power: Can charge objects, rendering them immune to conventional physics (mass, friction, etc).
Comments: I actually don't want to read the wiki for her since she seems like a major character in Ward, but yeah this power is strong. Perhaps the strongest, in terms of raw damage-dealing potential.

Power: Can create telekinetically controlled flowers or firecrackers of light.
Comments: I liked what New Wave was doing, too bad the space worms were turning everyone into murder hobos.

Power: Can force people to follow actions if a set of conditions, or rules, are met.
Comments: Girl, mind control?!

Power: Creates "buds" with intricate crystals, capable of a large variety of effects. Enhanced awareness and processing of fine details.
Comments: This power is insanely good for how casually it was mentioned. The fine-detail sense alone makes her one of the best thinkers out there, and adding the sheer versatility of her buds makes her very strong in a wide array of situations. She probably isn't front-line fighter material, but as a support, she's second to very few.

Power: Turns into living fog that causes harm to those within.
Comments: This guy probably sucks to fight.

Power: Shoots concussive blasts that inflict targets with specific emotions. Can sense the emotions of those around him as auras.
Comments: For some reason, I don't like this guy, but his power set is fairly strong. I think I recall Tattletale saying that too many emotional blasts could be potentially debilitating, so he has some pretty solid ways of taking someone out of a fight.

Power: Capable of granting invincibility, enhanced strength, and a potentially lethal electric touch to other individuals.
Comments: I don't think he can use his power on himself, but he's still really strong. Particularly, the invincibility he grants is great, and even Alexandria was willing to stack it with her own. Being able to create a squad of mid-level Brutes to do whatever you want is good, regardless of what you personally can do. Also, great name.

Power: Case 53, living mass of tentacles that grab and crush independently of her will.
Comments: Despite having fairly solid stats, this power sucks. Poor Sveta.

Power: Power reduces incoming damage to a set amount.  Imbues melee weapons with vast impact, but power protects the target, resulting in the target being thrown great distances.
Comments: This guy was cool as hell. Putting hands (or hammers) on Scion in an extended fight is one of the best feats in the entire series. I really like the idea of him smacking around criminals after giving them invincibility and having them crash into stuff. I less like the whole ruthless, family-targeting, reckless cop thing he's got going on.

Power: Enters catatonic state to create and then control a body, initial components decided by her, remainder by passenger.
Comments: I need some of whatever's in that Deus vial because both of the powers we've some come from it are absolutely cracked. Being able to mimic biological functions (or really, any physical function so long as she includes a biological basis) is insanely strong. The only real drawback to this power is that she needs time to adapt her forms to new situations, and can't do so too many times.

Power: Created streams of rubble, was stronger if rubble is already existent.
Comments: Entirely guessed, since he wasn't actually shown in the story. RIP.

Power: Teleporter who can only teleport to areas set up in advance.
Comments: That's just teleporting but kinda bad? Honestly, the Red Hands kind of don't deserve their cool group concept for how bad their powers are (or maybe that's the point idk).

Power: Cryogenics and stasis tinker.
Comments: This specialization is probably really good. In fact, we see Snowmann do some cool stuff with basically the same thing, but we only see Glace's tech used to keep the 9/000 asleep for a while.

Power: Can harvest the powers of a parahuman by either touching them, killing them instantly in the process, or by being in the general vicinity when they die. She can then manifest the harvested powers as spectral recreations of the original owner of the power, and can direct them to use their powers at will. These manifestations must stay in her immediate area, and the individual power of each specter decreases with the number of other specters summoned, not including specters that were duplicated with a different power. The spectres interact with the physical world as normal matter would, and will slowly regenerate if damaged or destroyed. Can also sense powers at great distances and through barriers, and can figure out how powers work by examining them.
Comments: The world's strongest melodramatic theater kid. Probably stronger than Eidolon until he learned to recharge his power batteries, and is definitely the strongest cape after she harvests his powers.

Power: Emotion-affecting aura focused on awe/intimidation, flight, enhanced strength, and personal forcefield that blocks one heavy hit or a few seconds of sustained damage hit before taking 1-2 seconds to recharge.
Comments: I don't actually want to look at her Wiki page since I know she's a major (or main?) character of Ward, but she has a pretty strong classical superheroine powerset.

Power: Can push his limbs into surfaces, causing the same limb to sprout off a surface of the same material at a distance. The limbs can be a size of his choosing, with bigger limbs emerging slower than smaller ones, and can also be controlled as a limb normally would or set in place as a statue. Can also sense surfaces in the area made of the same material he is currently touching.
Comments: A very solid power that took him far in the story, and could probably make him one of the most reliably potent heroes in the story. Theo gets underestimated, but he had a good showing in the behemoth fight with the giant lightning rod hand, survived the intense cape hunting and endbringer fighting through the time skip, solo'ed several Slaughterhouse 9000 members, survived Ellisburg, survied a direct confrontation with Jack Slash, who pulled a crazy Hookwolf combo, and survived Gold Morning. Respect my boy!

Power: Has increased agility and perception, can grant extremity/body part enhanced power and invulnerability/power immunity.
Comments: For the longest time, I didn't register that Grace was her cape name, referring to the synonym for elegance or refinement, and instead thought that he was just using her given name. Anyways it's a solid power, but the fact that the invincibility doesn't extend to more than one limb at a time really screws her over.

Power: Flies, has a personal forcefield that glows brighter over time before detonating.
Comments: He went out like a G, RIP.

Power: Can trap those around him in manipulable time loops of pain at will. Undoes or teleports away from any damage taken/negative effects applied, with a very favorable view as to what counts as damage.
Comments: Unsurprising of me to say that this is probably the most terrifying power in the series. Explaining the stats, I counted his damage negation as endurance and gave him a +1 to manipulation for the sheer psychological toll going up against his power must impart.

Power: Case 53, is boneless, durable, and produces various slimes with differing effects.
Comments: I like this guy, he reminds me of my Ethics 101 professor. Which is maybe not the best compliment I could give my professor, but I liked the class. His abilities are versatile, and I'm sure we haven't seen the full catalog of slimes he can create.

Power: Produces smoke-like darkness that dampens sound and light, as well as various other forms of waves and radiation. Later borrows powers via thicker, heavier darkness.
Comments: It's hard being the only straight Undersider. As a power thief, he's kind of hard to rank, so I just rated him at his base with the caveat that any/all of these numbers could go higher in the case that he's got the right power to borrow.

Power: Enhanced strength, thick-skinned, terrakinetic Case 53. Also has a thinker awareness of the ground around her.
Comments: Another of the of the many done dirty by Cauldron.

Power: Super strength, reflects damage back onto the attacker.
Comments: She got killed so close to freedom, RIP.

Power: Enhanced strength and durability, and close proximity power nullification. Can teleport and leave behind clones, which can act independently for a couple of seconds before exploding into a cloud of blood and dust.
Comments: Hatchet Face x anyone else is already a pretty rough combination to go up against, but Oni Lee might be one of the best choices. Giving the guy who can already beat pretty much anyone at close-range ambush tactics was a great move on Bonesaw's part.

Power: Creates a five-foot wide halo that can be moved at will, sports a cutting edge, and generates forcefields and lasers. Can also use the halo to fly.
Comments: Haven is honestly a very nice world-building addition, though this guy's powerset is a little underwhelming.

Power: Enhanced strength and durability, has an aura that nullifies the powers of those nearby.
Comments: For some reason, I thought he literally had a hatchet coming out of his face, like a Chainsaw Man character. He's definitely one of the strongest melee fighters in the series, by sheer virtue of making his opponents just regular people against a hulk. The fact that he's fast enough to get non-movers into his range too really helps too. I can honestly see him beating Eidolon in the right circumstances, though probably not Contessa.

Power: Can dramatically affect the emotions of those within his line of sight, and the effects can be long-term/permanent.
Comments: Line of sight mind control given to a horrible person? Yeah someone definitely needed to take this man's life.

Power: Hemokinetic with personal biokinesis.
Comments: Worst blood manipulator ever???

Power: Wavelength and frequency tinker.
Comments: The OG tinker, my favorite power classification. Wavelength manipulation is a good chunk of what made Scion so unbeatable, so a tinker with that specialty with very few restrictions on his shard would of course be very strong. Manton took him from us too soon, he almost definitely deserves to be ranked higher but I gotta go off of what we know he made.

Power: Can transform into a mass of blades and metal objects, the of which is form roughly customizable.
Comments: Unfortunately, this guy is a powerhouse, even though he's actively nerfing himself by only taking the shape of a wolf. His biggest strengths are his durability and his ability to recover from any injury he takes in his blade form so long as his "core" isn't destroyed, kind of like Echidna. Being able to recover from being torn in half by an Enbringer is crazy, and that's on top of being naturally bulletproof regardless of his form.

Power: Super strength/durability, resistance to long-range powers, and explosions on contact with attacks and attackers.
Comments: One of the best defensive abilities in the series, as she was able to tank Behemoth bolts and put her hands on Scion. Her mix of powers work really well together, making her a strong melee fighter that you just can't take down at range.

Power: Has a dimensional "flickering" ability that allows it to heal itself, escape restraints, and conjure items on one limb at a time. Also has wide-range aerokinesis, allowing it to maintain a large vacuum around itself and fly.
Comments: I actually have no clue if it's able to choose different abilities or if it just has the aerokinesis and flickering. On one hand, Taylor says that it had "chosen its powers", but on the other, we never see it switch them out, even when it'd be helpful. I ranked this on the idea that it could not do switch powers, but if it can, you can pretty much just give it 10s across the board like Eidolon.

Power: Affects memories about herself in a way that makes her completely unnoticable and unrecallable. Also decays records of herself over time. Memories made in abstract relation to her or made with senses that are not standard to humans are not affected.
Comments: Whoever this person is, she's one of the prime examples that PRT threat level != raw potency of an ability. She's a fantastic stranger, and that secondary ability she showed of being able to make people specifically forget the last couple moments of conversating with her has great applications in interrogation.

Power: Alters and augments powers, but also alters the psychology of the victim, in a deleterious fashion. Can also change her own personality into that of the target's "ideal woman".
Comments: Her powers kind of just suck for everyone involved, I feel bad for her.

Power: Materialized spears/blades/weights blades at high altitudes and minor ferrokinesis allowed some aiming of the falling objects.
Comments: I wonder if there's a connection between racism and metal-based powers. Extremely funny to imagine her trying to drop anvils on people's heads like Wile E. Coyote.

Power: Can extend a blade's cutting edge nigh-infinitely. Also subconsciously predicts/manipulates other parahumans, sabotaging them such that they will not succeed when working against him.
Comments: You can qualify, you can quibble, you can tack on extra powers, but Jack doesn't lose to parahumans.  Put me in the ring with him though I swear I'll lay him out.

Power: Creates orbs of light that redirect movement in the immediate vicinity.
Comments: This power kind of sucks ngl. It hinders allies as much as it does adversaries, and the hazard zones it makes are both not very impactful and highly visible.

Power: Radiation blaster, could focus effects to turn individuals into docile (to him) mutants, driven largely by cannibalistic hunger and fear of being irradiated further. Could make these mutants from the dead, animals, and humans.
Comments: This guy doesn't appear in the story aside from a one-off line, but we kinda know his powerset and that he achieved notoriety.  Tattletale said he was a tinker, but on this document, Wildbow said he was a blaster, but both have the same effect so pick your favorite. In any case, having an army of ghouls and super mutants on top of shooting cancer beams is probably a very good setup if you're a supervillain.

Power: Conducts various effects along his lance. Attacks must hit to trigger effects. Secondary mover ability in the form of a short burst of speed.
Comments: He's what I imagine Fire Emblem characters are like, having not played Fire Emblem. One of the few strikers whose power is tied to a specific item/type of items, kind of like Jack. The effects he generates all seem pretty useful, though needing to hit with his lance to trigger them (especially the suction one) kind of sucks.

Power: Can make one person she targets freeze in place, so long as she also stays still.
Comments: I think school of enchantment wizards gets this at level 2. This is good for locking down the one guy that you really don't want to be active, but she'd better have a good team behind her to back her up.

Power: Can produce all manner of metal shapes and objects from any solid surface. The material can come out in large amounts or very quickly, with an inverse relationship between the amount of metal being generated and the speed at which it comes out.
Comments: The power lottery was very kind to this guy. He's manton limited, so no generating metal inside of people, but nothing stops him from shooting spears from the ground or nearby walls directly into people. That also means he can generate armor around his allies whenever he wants. He's extremely versatile, and he can basically lock down a battlefield for anyone who can't get past solid iron/steel.

Power: Aerokinetic that is capable of forming whirlwinds and slicing people with air compressed into razorlike ribbons.
Comments: I don't know how good this was for the original user, but it must have sucked when Tohu got this.

Power: Jailbroken Administrator shard capable of controlling sapient creatures within sixteen feet of the host, and maintained more crude control of bugs at a range of 300 feet. Can co-opt the senses and gain awareness of the emotions, powers, and memories of the controlled creatures. Rapidly degrades the host's mental faculties.
Comments: The Scion-killer. What more could you want? It was the perfect tool for winning Gold Morning, but it's probably not a powerset I'd want normally, given that it's coupled with the loss of any ability to communicate and all.

Power: Tinker specializing in modular devices, or ones with alternate settings or uses. Struggles to build more focused devices.
Comments: Chris had a really nice little arc in the story, it was nice to see him grow into his own, though his therapy session was worrying.He's one of those Tinkers who's less defined by what he builds and more by how he builds it. That's a recipe for a strong and versatile tinker, à la Armsmaster or String Theory.

Power: Enhanced strength and endurance. Transfers wounds, damage, and effects to those he's touched in the last day, either spread evenly throughout or targeted on a single person.
Comments: Imagine fighting this guy, he touches you, and now you kinda just gotta leave him alone till tomorrow unless you want to die. That has to be rough.

Power: Healer, can create phantom limbs.
Comments: As a biomedical engineering student, I'm jealous. The healing aspect of the ability is fairly limited to the somewhat specific injury of losing a limb, but the general use of phantom limbs is good enough. The scene with his second trigger was crazy, I wonder how powerful he would have been had he survived.

Power: Wide range kinetic manipulation, more powerful closer to him, resulting in brute classification.
Comments: Did we even see this guy in action?

Power: Self-replication cape, clones could self-replicate. Reined in by psychological drawback.
Comments: Was probably the single worst cape to have near Echidna.

Power: Specializes in tinker drugs that turn subjects into monsters while storing their original state for when the transformation ends.
Comments: I kind of like him, the versatility of turning people into various monsters brings a lot to a fight, and could potentially let normal humans get in on the action. The fact that the transformations act like a Druid's wildshape in terms of damage is really great and can be lifesaving in a lot of situations. (Note: That last sentence was written before the nerf from the 2024 PHB :/).

Power: Can massively manipulate surroundings by tapping into alternate universes, selectively phasing whole sections or items into her environment.
Comments: The reality warper that was used to bully Scion. Probably has some of the most "breadth" and "depth" with her shard, as Bonesaw described it.

Power: Can fly, shoot energy beams, and project forcefields.
Comments: Simple, well-rounded power. Not much unique going on here, but it's solid.

Power: Can fly, project weak forcefields, and shoot precisely controlled, powerful energy beams.
Comments: More powerful lasers bring her closer to Legend, the premier laser-user of the verse. The forcefields are weak enough to be shattered by regular guys, but honestly, the better beams and flight compared to Lady Photon more than makes up for it.

Power: Tinker with no limits, except that creations have a chance to misfire respective to how much he's worked on the idea/in the field. He cannot make the same thing twice.
Comments: I actually have a lot to say about this guy, because he's one of the biggest cases of missed potential in the series that often gets way underestimated by the fandom. I won't rehash it all here, but u/Indepentent-Height87 does a great job of explaining what I imagine a good L33t to look like. In any case, his highest ratings here mainly come from his "Warp Signal Shooter" and "Reality Hacker" devices, which both do limited high-tier reality warping. Those two alone are individually contenders for the most powerful tinker device in the series, and the fact that L33t could make them and still not succeed as a cape is a testament to how woefully incompetent he is.

Power: A flying laser-blaster with a large toolbox of possible types of laser and laser effects. Has a breaker state made of living light, which he reflexively shifts to avoid damage and absorb energy to restore his regular body to peak condition.
Comments: I didn't actually think he was super powerful while I was reading, but once I realized how versatile his lasers were, it became clear why he was considered one of the top 3 heroes in-universe. Unless you can somehow counter everything he can throw at you or aim-dodge highly maneuverable beams of light, you're not beating him. The reaction-time defensive breaker state and the fastest flight in the series are just the cherries on top. Also, he probably single-handedly spurred on a lot of progress for gay rights on Earth Bet, so he's goated for that.

Power: Spear wielder with the ability to distort spear and convey self to target point.
Comments: I'm not sure what the power description given here means, but all we saw him do in the story was extend his spear to hit a child and then immediately get knocked out by a "child", so it can't be that good.

Power: Capable of hearing everything nearby, as well as teleporting himself to areas within earshot of a given utterance.
Comments: This is one of those sets of powers that made me think "wow Wildbow is so creative" while I was reading it.

Power: Creates portals to otherworldly ocean depths, resulting in massive geysers that can withdraw to suck in waterborne items/enemies, and can close the portals to leave those sucked in in another reality.
Comments: Having the chance to banish people to the ocean depths of another dimension is insanely good, the fact that she can compel their movement into the portals if they don't have a way to escape strong water currents makes this ability really strong against physically weaker parahumans, but kind of bad against stronger parahumans who can resist the effects.

Power: Aura regenerates those in the area.
Comments: One of the few healers of Worm, and one that is surprisingly healing specific. Most of the other healers in the series really only heal as a side effect of what they do, or they can only work with something specific, or their healing just kind of sucks in general. But this guy kind of just has strong healing with no downsides, maybe making him the most straight-up "healer" healer in the series.

Power: Gains increasingly powerful strength, durability, healing, armor, senses, additional physical features, and pyrokinesis, so long as he keeps fighting.
Comments: The dragon himself. I didn't actually like him that much, but once I read his backstory interlude I started to see the appeal. One of the few parahumans who can singlehandedly go toe-to-toe with an endbringer, if only temporarily after some build-up. That makes him objectively one of the strongest in the series, which I'm pretty sure is something that is generally agreed upon.

Power: Massive breaker form fueled by draining from the physical and mental faculties of those nearby.
Comments: Tragically imprisoned for girlbossing, many such cases.

Power: Tinker specializes in closed systems, typically augmentations for a multi-piece suit containing his own body.
Comments: One of the most compelling characters in the series, and my favorite S9 member. The evocative way in which his appearance and movements are described, the way his backstory explains but can't justify why he's become what he's become, the inversion of his powers when he went from being a protector of the people to a self-isolating sociopath, pretty much everything about the character is just perfectly written. In the first Slaughterhouse 9 arc, he felt like the most terrifying member, and that's saying something next to Bonesaw.

Power: Electromagnetic forcefield, used to augment own strength.
Comments: That sure is a brute, not much else to say.

Power: Case 53. Blocks out sensory aspects of powers progressively more with proximity.
Comments: Hard-countering all thinkers in range, as well as a bunch of different aspects of a bunch of different powers, is insanely good. He's a great guy to have to support your team, but isn't all that great on his own, since you could just shoot him (or drop a disintegration knife on him) from outside his range.

Power: Skilled osteokinetic, produced and re-shaped a variety of bone constructions, could affect enemy's exposed bones.
Comments: If I were a cape, this guy would be on the top of my 'people I absolutely do not want to fight' list. He gets to freely control a strong-as-steel material, doing pretty much whatever he wants with it, and if he manages to injure you bad enough that he can see your bones, then you're basically cooked. Plus, he gets some solid regeneration powers, and his shard budded into the most versatile biokinetic in the multiverse, so I think the lack of pain tolerance from breaking bone is honestly a fair trade.

Power: Can mass produce tinkertech.
Comments: As a tinker who works with other people's tinker tech, he's hard to rank, but breaking one of the few restricting factors of tinkering is conceptually awesome. The problem is, we don't really see much of what he built, except for some extra nanothorn knives, combat engines, some kind of sword, and some laser pistols. All good stuff, but definitely not the best he could do. Still, I'll have to stick by my rules on judging tinkers by what they have done and not what they could do.

Power: Can unfold herself into ribbons. Absorbs others, gaining memories/traits at the cost of her own. Can layer, diluting her form with multiple consumed. Slowly digests those she absorbs, but can choose to hold them inside herself without absorbing them. Case 53.
Comments: I guess it's technically better than nothing, but this power sucks.

Power: Can turn invisible and undetectable, spewing off an odorless gas that causes a variety of bad mental effects, eventually ending in a coma with enough exposure. Being attacked in stranger form causes him to explode into thick, green smoke.
Comments: The descriptions given only say that the gas is odorless, but I'm going to make the assumption that they're also tasteless and colorless, or else there would be no point in being a stranger. In that case, he's good as a basically living gas grenade, but not that great in any other situation.

Power: Can call forth conventional weaponry, or modify the summoned weaponry at will.
Comments: GOD BLESS AMERICA WHAT THE FUCK IS A KILOMETER YEEE HAWWW. Also, guys remember when she was implied to have been making a nuke. I remember. Top tier.

Power: Can animate objects with a touch and command them. There is no upward size, number, or distance limit, but the objects are not autonomous and need to receive direct commands, larger objects need more time to animate, and nothing smaller than half the size of a typical car can be animated. Works much better on high-quality and mechanical objects, and multiple objects can be combined together at the cost of time and durability.
Comments: First of all, I really underestimated her powerset. It's complicated to deal with, but her power absolutely has the potential to be fairly broken in the right environment. Her powers are oddly loose on hard limits, so there's a lot that she can theoretically do with a little bit of time and effort. Like, there is definitely an alternate universe where she gets a tinker to make her a big robot and turns Worm into a mecha anime. We also saw in her fight as Mockshow that you can't really use objects to restrain her, as they'll just end up as one of her minions. Practical stuff like that combined with the raw firepower of her minions makes her an even better "big thing" master than Bitch or Parian imo. As a side note, I really loved her little Mockshow to Romp character arc, I'm glad Wildbow brought her back like that.

Power: Controls a massive, smoke-like pet that reacts to malicious intent and gets bigger and stronger as it eats more humans.
Comments: Managed to be a consistent front-liner against Scion, putting her in the upper echelon of capes by default. I believe it was said that Africa in general was a pretty brutally competitive continent for capes, so her being at the top of that totem pole says a lot about her relative strength.

Power: Could teleport to tagged people or objects.  Had lesser powers including increased coordination and agility.
Comments: I like this character way too much given the amount of screen time she gets. RIP Mouse Protector you and Aisha would have been best friends.

Power: Hyper-skilled, emphasizing teamwork and cooperation, secondary power enabled emotion reading.
Comments: I think we see him once and he doesn't even really use his powers.

Power: Hybrid of Mouse Protector & Ravager. Agile, flexible, knife-fingers, wounds inflicted scar badly, smoke. Can teleport to the side of anyone they wound.
Comments: Mouse Protector didn't deserve this :(

Power: Formed a hulking body of detritus, moisture, and particulate matter, with human form at the center.
Comments: I kinda get why he joined the Merchants I'd be mad too if this was my power.

Power: Carried several pocket dimensions, flinging them out and activating them for a selection of dimension-specific effects.
Comments: He might be my favorite (human) PRT member everything about him is so cool and honestly I needed more screentime from him, so he's probably the most unfortunate Echinda victim in my eyes. His powers basically make him Eidolon-lite, with some pretty crazy versatility while still being powerful. Also, he gets to dress like a wizard and chant spells so he's automatically top-tier.

Power: Can generate forcefields that can be finely moved and maniplated, or shot at high velocities. Can also create forcefields within living beings, violating the Manton effect.
Comments: Taylor described her so impressively (and gayly) on their first meeting in Extermination so I was waiting to see what she could do. She proceeded to fuck Leviathan up, so she was immediately one of the coolest characters in the series to me. I was always waiting for her to come back, but she didn't get too much else, aside from appearing briefly during GM. I do hear she comes back in Ward though, so I'm glad.

Power: Can create materials/goods. Can track the location of the things he creates.
Comments: I mean, I don't think we actually see him use his power, so it's not very easy to rank.

Power: Case 53, can adhere to surfaces, has hallucinogenic bodily fluids, is physically strong/tough/agile, and has a prehensile tail.
Comments: The closest we get to Spider-man in Worm. He's got a fairly well-rounded powerset, and I can see how he makes a good addition to Faultline's crew.

Power: Forces all others to regard him as friendly and non-threatening, regardless of his actions
Comments: Genuinely if you're a hero and don't want to risk killing civilians how do you even catch this guy? I think this stranger power is pretty much on par with Aisha's because there seem to be fewer ways around it but he also has to be consciously targeting someone for it to work.

Power: When not being observed, transforms into a strong, fast, and durable monster. Transformation resets body to perfect condition.
Comments: This is honestly such a cool power, and I especially liked the tactics that she used in conjunction with it. Her monster form is obviously powerful, but the fact that she basically forces the other side to pay special attention to her at all times is insanely beneficial in cape fights that often involve some degree of subterfuge.

Power: Has a breaker state that can enter and "infect" surrounding material, allowing it to be controlled at will and further spread infection. Can reform from the infected ground if killed, and doesn't need to eat, sleep, or end breaker state.
Comments: We know very little about how her power actually operates, but she seems like a kinda Tohu-lite. She can resurrect herself, and her presence during the S-9000 arc was explicitly making things a lot harder during some battles, so she must have been good at what she did.

Power: Creates custom minions after recycling existing living matter. Minions can procreate and have powers of their own.
Comments: An S-class threat to rival the Endbringers. The fact that the PRT was forced to leave him alone should be proof enough of the effectiveness of his power, but he does have his weaknesses. If his shard hadn't made him stay in one spot, I think it's very possible that he could have taken over the world.

Power: Cam produce harmless gas that could be shaped into controlled but fragile silent images.
Comments: Trolled an entire audience by picking the same name as her sister, ggs.

Power: Can split and delegate powers amongst a group.
Comments: I mean I guess it's a good power if you're a part of something like the Yàngbǎn, but a power that needs a bunch of other allied capes to be good kinda sucks.

Power: Can produce toxic gas that could be shaped into controlled but fragile silent images.
Comments: Of these two sisters, one of them joined the most evil and vile group in the series, and the other joined the Slaughterhouse 9.

Power: Slowly shifts body into becoming personally attractive. Can also learn skills at an enhanced rate.
Comments: Honestly? It's not the worst power on here. It'd be more useful IRL than a lot of even the higher-scored powers on this spreadsheet.

Power: Brainwashing-capable thinker.
Comments: Doesn't even appear in the story, these scores are complete guesses based on the single-sentence description we get of them.

Power: Teleports, leaving behind a duplicate that can act autonomously for 5-10 seconds before bursting into dust.
Comments: One of the coolest takes on a teleporter I've seen in fiction and was one of the first characters that really got me invested in Wildbow's worldbuilding. Take note that Jack says something about it giving him brain damage, but that's never actually confirmed, and Jack isn't actually that smart.

Power: Can grant temporary powers with a touch. Powers include invincibility, super strength/speed, pyrokinesis, regeneration, and flight.
Comments: I constantly get her confused with Othello. Kind of like with tinkers, this is a ranking based on the powers she can provide to others because she can't actually give them to herself. However, all the powers are so solid that if the time limit on them wasn't so small (30 seconds to 2 minutes) she could be considered one of the best trumps in the series. With consistent contact, she kind of is already.

Power: Has an invisible, untouchable mirror self in a mirror world, which relays the information it gathers. Can switch worlds with the other self, mimicking teleportation, and can also allow the mirror self to interact weakly with objects in the real world.
Comments: Depending on the range at which the mirror can move and relay information, this is either the best stranger power in the series barring maybe Sleeper, or just pretty good.

Power: Touch-based biokinesis. Can sense the biology of any lifeform at a touch, and modify that biology at with a high degree of fidelity.
Comments: I love her and wish her the best, but Amy probably has the most wasted potential in terms of how she uses her power (which is admittedly entirely intentional on Wildbow's part). She basically has the strongest parts of every "wet" tinker and striker mixed together, plus the ability to make custom lifeforms. Earth Bet lucked out when she didn't decide to become a S9 member since her + Bonesaw is basically a world-ending threat.

Power: Can manipulate lightweight objects with telekinesis. Can "fill" porous materials with telekinesis to make strong and durable puppets.
Comments: Apparently, the endbringer-level "true-use" of her power gets revealed in Ward but I haven't read it yet so she's stuck at this ranking for now. That's not to say she has it bad, remember she was significantly hampering Leviathan for a couple of moments in basically her first-ever fight.

Power: Dust tinker.
Comments: I mean he's the dust tinker, what else is there to explain?

Power: Can set a target's state and location back to how it was three seconds ago. This includes the memories of living things.
Comments: I'm sorry I hate him even more than I hate Teacher or Saint like WHY? Someone should have just shot him in the head bc clearly the Simurgh did an equivalent amount of brain damage.

Power: Creates portals that lead a couple of minutes back in time. Can lead light through two portals to massively increase its energy output over time, resulting in a powerful blast when released.
Comments: Being able to time travel a couple of minutes back is one of the best powers in the series on its own, but adding a country-destroying, endbringer-frying bomb on top of that? Yeah, he needed to be killed off.

Power: Can create walls over the course of several seconds.
Comments: It's possible that he also has enhanced strength, since Taylor pointed out how heavy his costume and equipment looked and he was moving just fine, but that's not explicitly said so I won't include it. Otherwise, we don't really get a lot from him.

Power: Can take over the bodies of others.
Comments: I honestly have no clue how to rank him, especially because we don't know how his power activates/operates.

Power: Can split self into three clones, and can optionally include items in the cloning process. Clones are identical and can survive destruction so long as one remains. Clones can be re-absorbed for a short-term boost to physicality.
Comments: She had a pretty cool fight with Skitter, and her power is pretty versatile. Just being able to turn into three regular people isn't that strong, though, and the strength/speed/durability boost could come in handy sometimes but isn't super significant. I'd say the best part of this power is that each of the clones counts as a "true self" so it doesn't really matter which ones die as long as one remains. This sets up some interesting tactics, to say the least.

Power: Interdimentional technology tinker, could also build more general tinker gadgets. Was in constant mental contact with himself from two different realities.
Comments: The most cartoon-villain-sounding cape name, I love it. He was dead before the story even started, but the fact that he's still fairly relevant well after his death is a major accomplishment on his part. His specific brand of tinkertech is honestly really hard to rank on top of not being directly shown for the most part, so the numbers will probably end up a little strange.

Power: Biokinetic durability and size, could turn people inside-out with a touch.
Comments: GET THE HELL OUT GET OUUUT GET OUUUUUT GET OUUUT -CalebCity

Power: Can turn people into controlled, wall-crawling beasts. The effect is fragile, with the victim returning to normal if the beast form is harmed.
Comments: As Theo pointed out, most of the effect of this power is more psychological than anything. Lots of parahumans have ways to pretty easily deal with the monsters, but having to deal with them all at once or else you leave room for the others to become victims? That's much harder.

Power: Can fly, and can charge up with light over time and shoot it back out as large, twisting blasts.
Comments: Goddamn does being racist give you a buff in this universe? The only saving grace is that I don't believe she has any sort of enhanced durability, so you can just shoot her.

Power: Flame manipulation, special effects, and gun tinker.
Comments: Okay look, I know I said I'd rank tinkers based on what they have made and not what they could feasibly make, but when we literally don't see anything a tinker has made I'm just gonna have to make some guesses.

Power: Can connect to nearby capes and combine the effects of their powers into bullets.
Comments: We only see one showing of her power, but it was enough to make Scion stumble so there's that. Regardless, her power isn't actually fully explained so this is the best I can give it.

Power: Enhanced physique, all wounds inflicted would fester and scar horrifically if not tended to, scratches becoming eviscerations; maintained clairvoyant awareness of wounded.
Comments: She sounds good in the way that grab-bag capes tend to be, with their versatility and smaller powers that synergize in big ways. Not a lot about this power helps directly in a fight, but it's fantastic for punishing retreats, which I imagined happened often in her time in the S9.

Power: Creates innumerable energy blasts and condenses them to devastating effect with a lens he projects and controls.
Comments: His entire screen time came while Taylor was blind, so our descriptions of him and his powers are vague. Tecton spoke highly of him though, and I trust Tecton.

Power: Can cause involuntary muscle movements in other's bodies. If given the time, can establish full bodily control, being able to experience their senses and use their parahuman abilities. Can control multiple people, but fidelity worsens as the number and distance of puppets grows.
Comments: It's fucked that he had absolutely zero chance of ever living a normal life, but at least during his time with the Underesiders, he lived as he wanted to without committing heinous crimes, experienced genuine connection, and went out something of a hero. His spasming power is pretty potent and would be even better if he cared to train physically. His real master power's use is obviously great, even if using it to its fullest extent would make the entire cape community want to kill him.

Power: Can suction air into his mouth with enough force to pull objects towards himself, and can blow that air back out with a similarly potent force.
Comments: Lol, I guess you could say... I guess you could say this power... I guess you could say this power uhhh is bad?

Power: Can absorb energy with a lantern and launch it back as programmable energy orbs that can detonate or shear targets.
Comments: Revel is one of those characters that I wish we saw more of! Her power is flashy and interesting, not to mention potent and useful.

Power: Can increase the range of projectile weapons. Also has enhanced perceptions.
Comments: Cool power, but was never actually showcased and doesn't seem that useful at face value.

Power: Can fly, and can create compressed ice fractals that can curve in the air. Fractals explode into glaciers of predetermined size and shape on impact.
Comments: One of the more conventional powers in the series, but it's not a bad one. She ends up being a 'flying artillery' type of cape, like Legend or Purity, with less raw firepower but more battlefield control than they tend to get.

Power: Can break nonliving objects into small, sharp fragments that can be weakly telekinetically controlled. The fragments can be reconstituted into the original object, in whole or in parts.
Comments: For some reason, I thought this power was way better on my first reading than it ended up being. The fragments are too weak to kill even bugs, and her only real way of attacking is dropping big stuff on people, which can be dodged by anyone paying attention. I guess it's useful in that it destroys nonliving materials immediately, which is good for messing with the environment, but she would definitely struggle in a 1-on-1 fight.

Power: Can attune to discrete objects with momentary touch, allowing for telekinetic control of them afterward. Objects can weigh several tons, but attuning to any more than two causes negative side effects.
Comments: This feels worse than standard telekinesis to me. The high weight limit is nice, having to touch the object for several seconds first and being limited to two objects at a time removes a lot of what makes telekinesis a good power in the first place.

Power: Tinker 0, maintains an understanding of Dragon's tech via Teacher.
Comments: From his introduction, this guy really tricked me into thinking he'd be cool as hell with his names, backstory, and general vibe. And then not only does it get revealed that he's a powerless bum, but he keeps trying to kill my favorite character and messes things up in an end-of-the-world scenario completely unnecessarily. That makes me Saint's biggest hater, and I promise you that if I saw him in the streets irl, I would lay his ass out I'm so serious.

Power: Can sense people based on their emotions, and is innately aware of people's mental defenses and how to break them.
Comments: At face value, this seems like the most lackluster power of Heartbreaker's kids. We haven't seen it in action though, so maybe it's better than I'm thinking.

Power: Can manipulate blood.
Comments: Was stated by Taylor to be able to hurt Leviathan in some way, but I don't see how that would happen so I don't know if I can trust that information. The only thing we see this character do is heal, which is rare enough in the Wormverse to be pretty valuable on its own.

Power: Can shapeshift into other people, and produce clones capable of the same.
Comments: We don't really know the details of how he controls his clones or how far he can be from them, but even so, his powers are perfect for the life of subterfuge he's taken up. Like most Vegas capes, this won't help him much in a straight-up fight though beyond giving him a numbers advantage.

Power: Can grant others the enhanced ability to read into verbal and non-verbal cues, allowing the recipient to draw conclusions about the thoughts and brain patterns of others.
Comments: The closest we get to straight-up telepathy in the Wormverse, I believe. This is basically if Tattletale was optimized towards guessing people's (and Endbringer's) thoughts, which is notably hard for her.

Power: Can spatially twist matter until it dissolves into radioactive dust.
Comments: Giving your opponent cancer even if you lose is devious.

Power: Can press self and others into 2D space, hiding them on surfaces. Damaging the surface damages the person inside.
Comments: This is helpful if you're defending an area that you know will stay relatively undamaged and want to pull off a surprise attack, but otherwise, I'm not seeing too much use.

Power: Can transfer physical and mental afflictions to and from himself. The target must stay within 150 feet and not sustain more damage for 1-6 hours for the effect to hold.
Comments: The GOAT part of his name comes from him giving us one of the best lines in the series.

Power: Can manipulate sound with a high level of fidelity to pull of feats like imitating voices, broadcasting across wide areas, nullifing sounds and voices, amplifying a sound to be painful to hear, and spreaking to others as if she were the voice in thier head. Can transmit sound through many accousitc mediums, and can hear any sound within her radius.
Comments: I think this ability is my favorite out of the Slaughterhouse 9, even if it doesn't belong to my favorite character. It's just so useful at such a wide range, basically breaking down the communication of any team that gets anywhere near you, whilst also being able to spy on them and fuck up their ability to focus on much of anything.

Power: Allows those connected to them to communicate and handle information overload during thinker visions.
Comments: Does one job, and does it well. Not much to say about this one.

Power: Chaotically generates spherical bursts that remove non-structural components of nearby matter from existence. The bursts will ignore most defenses and can be pushed to happen more frequently in a given general direction.
Comments: This is proof that not all all-or-nothing powers are particularly good. Not being able to aim his blasts at all makes this a great power to potentially take chunks out of teammates and completely miss adversaries. And in reality, if he doesn't manage to hit whatever he needs to, he's screwed because the power offers him literally nothing other than a strong attack.

Power: Can violently draw in moisture in a tight cone or personal field, rapidly dehydrating targets.
Comments: This is definitely one of the funnier "fuck you" powers in the series. Imagine it's a hot day and you're fighting this guy in a thick costume and he hits you I'd be so mad if I survived.

Power: Can shift self and touched objects into a shadow state, making them light, intangible, nearly invisible, and vulnerable to electric shock.
Comments: I couldn't help but think this girl was just a drama queen the whole story, I think she needed a normal outlet for her edginess, like hop on Reddit or something. Her power is fairly alright, it does well as a mover ability and lets her ignore some defenses, but her intangibility really isn't all that potent compared to some of the other demonstrations of the same power we see in the series.

Power: Case 53, utilizes telekinesis and clairvoyance at a microscopic level to emulate impossible levels of good fortune. Is also a blindspot for other clairvoyants/precognatives.
Comments: This is like baby's first Path to Victory, in that things are just going to go right for Shamrock, which goes a long way in the Wormverse. She's well-defended by her power on her own, but this also makes her a fantastic addition to any team.

Power: Uses ultrasonic frequencies to manipulate silicon, with more fine control the closer/more silicon there is to work with. Can also hear through silicon materials.
Comments: AoE god, I wish she would have come up more in the S9K arc. Her power is insanely versatile in a way that's strangely similar to Taylor's. The more solid nature of what she controls makes her fantastic at making barriers and shutting down areas while still being able to pack enough punch to shear Hookwolf in half if she needs to. She's honestly such an underrated part of what made the S9 so terrifying, and she's also responsible for giving us one of the best Taylor characterization moments in the form of her running to save her dad. Thanks, Shatterbird! Also, I know her "song" can't actually be heard but I always imagine it as "A Beautiful Song" from the Nier:Automata soundtrack.

Power: Thinker, maintained expert tactical abilities and understanding of movements and maneuvers, giving a pseudo-clairvoyance regarding battlefields.
Comments: We only really saw a direct showing of his power against Kephri, where he was able to launch a quick but ineffective counter-attack against her.

Power: Can fly, create lasers, and project forcefields.
Comments: RIP, I liked the dynamic the powers within New Wave had. I think the shields are the most useful of the three powers they get, so I would have liked to see more of this guy before he died.

Power: Can teleport sections of a landscape. Limited accuracy/control.
Comments: This power seems hard to use directly in combat, but being able to restructure the terrain like this is a pretty huge boon, so I'd say it's pretty good.

Power: Generates an unstoppable and immovable projection that can share it's properties with people and objects it maintains contact with
Comments: I needed a little more elaboration on this backstory because I feel like there was a whole can of unexplored worms here.

Power: Can spatially lock the relative positions and orientations of people, objects, and effects.
Comments: This power on its own is insanely useful, even accounting for the fact that touch is the preferred method of activating it. With it, you can basically stop yourself and others from ever being touched by any one thing, and in a world where so many rely on touch or physical objects to dish out damage, that makes you really hard to beat to a lot of people. Combine that with the whole linear velocity =  angular velocity * radius thing from high school physics and you can get some insane output from a guy with a ball and a big rock.

Power: Able to lay strips of power that multiply others' speed.
Comments: We see very little of how this power works, but we do know that it's instrumental for getting everyone where they need to be on time, which makes it pretty instrumental in the story overall.

Power: Can cover surfaces with a field of unidirectional repulsive force. The fields can be up to ~100 yards on either side and can be layered to increase the severity of the effect.
Comments: This is kind of up there with The Hand from JJBA, where an okay power is kneecapped by being used by a complete idiot, and in response, the fandom way overstates the ability itself. Yes, a smarter Skidmark could feasibly knock around endbringers, but at the end of the day, the power is slow and not all that potent without significant wind-up.

Power: Regenerator capable of manipulating his own skin. Can extend the ability by attaching the skin of others to his own.
Comments: I wonder if there are any potential capes that get powers like this, that make them do shit like staple other people's skin to their own to be effective, and just sit out of the cape business entirely bc fuck that.

Power: Can telepathically control and communicate with bugs. The number of bugs she can control is unlimited within her range, which encompasses a several-block radius. Her commands dominate her minion's instincts entirely, and she can make them perform complex/self-destructive actions, with commands staying in effect until she actively dismisses them.
