She can give general instructions to or finely control any number of individual bugs in her swarm at once, and may accidentally command them unconsciously, such as by naturally offloading her body language onto them. She can get sensory input from her minions, which is individually difficult for her human brain to interpret but can be used as a swarm to get a clearer picture. She also innately knows the biologies and relative positions of all bugs within her area of control
Comments: I could go on for hours talking about how narratively perfect this powerset is for Taylor, but for the sake of not writing an essay in a spreadsheet, I'll keep it to the meatheaded power scaling. Besides making her locally omniscient, Taylor's little army is also so diverse and versatile that the amount of things she can do at any given moment is staggering. I mean biting, envenoming, sticking up with webs, blocking vision, blocking airways, communicating, dragging, and freaking out anyone within a several-block range is fantastic, and being able to do all those at once to as many people as you like just makes her power almost busted. I honestly don't think I rated her high enough on any given thing, I may have to pull a Tagg and give her a +2 to every stat just because I genuinely cannot predict what she'll think of next.

Power: Fx nk N\’i wjfqqd uzy xtrjymnsl mjwj. Ktw wjfq.
Tinker specialized in closed systems, with a heavy focus on the use of cold and ice. Can also slow the movements of people and objects within a certain range.
Comments: We don't know how much of Winter's power is in this clone, but it wasn't brought up during the fight where it was around so it can't be that effective. I like this take on a mannequin clone though, the things it made seemed cool and the name is fantastic.

Power: Can forcefully spit a chemical that ignites intensely on contact with people or objects.
Comments: I think this is the first character whose existence I genuinely forgot about.

Power: Can rapidly generate clones, which degrade to the point of being braindead in seconds and a meaty pulp within 15 minutes.
Comments: Cool power but not super useful beyond creating meat shields and causing a disruption.

Power: Can generate orbs that have a disintegration effect, or alternative sweep over a target for a limited clairvoyant effect.
Comments: His power is honestly unclear, so I don't know if this is an accurate ranking.

Power: Precog whose effectiveness increases with chaos and emotionality.
Comments: I mean that's a pretty cool power but I doubt he's in enough situations with mass chaos to make this version of precognition better than the standard, non-chaos-affected precogs on average.

Power: Vehicle tinker, focused on adding size, augmentations, and additions to the vehicles. Can intuitively drive/pilot anything made.
Comments: I could honestly see her being a threat if she were in a better situation. Vehicles is a solid tinker spec on its own, but she also seems to be able to make whatever she wants to mount on the vehicle, which is very versatile and could be really be anything.

Power: Tinker specializing in propulsion, mainly jetpacks and missiles.
Comments: Never actually appears in the story aside from a mention that he stole a potential Taylor villain name and his tech was used as the basis for Taylor's jetpack.

Power: Aerokinetic. Can compress air into claws that can detonate explosively at will, carry sounds and smells to himself, deflect projectiles with blasts of air and strong currents, and lift himself into a limited flight.
Comments: Another one of the few characters that I completely forgot about. But once again we got another annoyingly solid power from E88. This one really has a little bit of everything, and is honestly pretty fantastic on the versatility front.

Power: Capable of mass teleportation.
Comments: Explicitly the best mass teleporter (at least, after Doormaker).

Power: Tinker favoring macro-scale doomsday devices, creates objects that have timers built in from the start of the construction process, with catastrophic misfires if interrupted.
Comments: I love my wife, she has endbringers canonically scared enough to be avoiding her and blasted the strongest thing on the planet into the stratosphere.

Power: Capable of manifesting parts of his body from various dimensions, resulting in more powerful attacks at a greater range
Comments: Poor guy, but the scene where he gets bodied by The Number Man is so cool.

Power: Can create balls of intense heat and light of varying sizes. The miniature suns can be controlled and scaled at will. Is also immune to intense heat and light from all sources.
Comments: Having an orb that kills most things is great, don't get me wrong, but I think a part of this power that people overlook is the fact that the mini sun produces blinding light. Whenever Mars is in a fight, she can pick a direction and say "yeah not only can you not be over here without getting fried, you don't even get to look over here anymore" and I think that's fun.

Power: Can intuitively extrapolate large amounts of data from a given source.
Comments: One of my favorite powers in the series, this is the most creative take on the standard but difficult-to-satisfyingly-write "super intelligence" power that I've ever seen. Wildbow really hit it out of the park with the power concept alone, and when you attach it to a character as fun and interesting as Lisa, it becomes something really fantastic. From an in-story effectiveness perspective, Lisa is one of the best Thinkers we're aware of, in a way that I don't think the spreadsheet is really capturing. A good chunk of what makes her power so good is that nobody can tell wtf it is, so they really can't prepare for it properly, which I can't truly reflect with a numerical value.

Power: Grants tinker and thinker powers to those he touches, at the cost of recipients' personal autonomy.
Comments: He seems like he's going to be an important character in Ward, so I don't want to spoil myself on him just yet by reading his Wiki page. However, from what we see in Worm, he was able to become a major player just after getting out of super jail and stay relevant after GM, so it's safe to say that his power's probably good. This rating probably isn't high enough ngl.

Power: Tinker specializing in power armor and short-range weapons/tools, with a Thinker ability revolving around understanding architecture, seismic activity, and geology.
Comments: He's such a cool guy, I liked the bit where he talked about how he thought about power synergies. Great leader, stand up guy, and has a pretty potent powerset that effectively makes him a Brute, Shaker, and maintains his Thinker status all at once.

Power: Nearly intangible, capable of affecting surroundings through mass self-replication.
Comments: Another fantastically creative Wildbow power. I wish we got to see more of her in general.

Power: A near-perfect calculator. Effectively makes him omniscient regarding anything he observes that could be given a numerical value.
Comments: My favorite of Cauldron's capes, and undeniably the best thinker that isn't named Contessa or Clairvoyant. He's good in combat, but his ability to manipulate the world economy is probably so much more useful.

Power: Can selectively force those around her into a sleep-like trance, though doing so affects herself as well. The trance can be broken with a jarring action.
Comments: LOL this power SUCKS.

Power: Produced shaker waves that altered terrain within their scope, raising, restructuring, or leveling buildings.
Comments: Obviously useful, though not for the conflict-based situations that capes usually find themselves in.

Power: Can alter the direction of the gravitational pull on himself and others, as well as on objects and the environment.
Comments: This is a way better power than I assumed at first, I didn't pick up on the fact that he was making it work on specific objects and people. It pretty much amounts to a finicky form of telekinesis, which is a good power regardless.

Power: A power suit tinker with a suit the size of a small building.
Comments: This guy was built to be a cartoon evil scientist-type character and would 100% be one if they were ever on screen.

Power: Case 43, uses pseudopod-like limbs to generate power for tinker equipment. Tinker with a specialization on quickly building crude yet durable armor and weapons at a very low resource cost.
Comments: I never realized this guy was a Case 53. Making complicated things to face whatever you need is the whole strength of a tinker, so this specialization that tries to mitigate the time/resource weakness of most tinkers by getting rid of that strength really just ends up as a kind of sucky brute power instead.

Power: Can swap the positions of any two objects or people within field of view if they are of similar mass. Differences in mass are balanced by adding the mass of the air around the smaller object. Can sense the size/mass/density of the things he's using his power on.
Comments: It's hard to overstate how useful this ability is for manipulating a battlefield, a fact that was handily showcased when Krouse managed to screw up a whole field of capes, including EIDOLON.

Power: Enhanced athleticism and durability. Can control the force and area of effect of his voice to shape it into a concussive blast. Also has a mild regenerative ability.
Comments: I thought his power was better when I was first reading, but now it does kind of make sense how badly he got beat by Taylor. He's good as one of the most direct victims of Taylor doing her thing, but he's not much of a character otherwise.

Power: Enhances the powers of nearby parahumans.
Comments: More power = more better. I don't really see how she'd be useful outside the Yàngbǎn, besides helping out artillery capes from the back.

Power: Can transfer damage to anyone who had been previously touched within a certain time frame, and has a short-range power nullifying aura.
Comments: Bonesaw says she's bad with names, but she keeps giving out some pretty good ones. This mashup plays to Hatchet Face's strengths, though not quite as well as Hack Job does. Still, and even more invincible Hatchet Face would suck to fight so bad.

Power: Can maintain high-level mastery of any specific technique for as long as he maintains focus on them.
Comments: This power is way worse than I was thinking since I thought he gained mastery of full fields of skills rather than specific techniques. At best, he can be above average at a few specific things at once and has to constantly swap between them to be useful.

Power: Creates bear-shaped forcefields.
Comments: If the bears were real, she'd be able to beat Jack Slash. But as of now, her power is kind of unclear. The cast list lays that she makes bear-shaped forcefields, but Taylor implies that she duplicates herself and her duplicates shapeshift into forcefield bears? I'll assume the former, but the latter would change some of the numbers.

Power: Provided single-target power immunity to others.
Comments: OMG, I've got Confessions to make, and U Don't Have to Call me out on this, but I completely forgot about this guy.

Power: Can implant suggestions and instructions in those he makes eye contact with.
Comments: This power is actually pretty ridiculous, the fact that he can set up hypnotic triggers for later is insanely busted, basically making him a mini-simurgh without the pre/retrocognition. Plus, he gets a thinker ability that lets him track even people like Aisha. Or at least he did, idk if his powers even still work after the whole maggots thing.

Power: Super strength/reflexes that scale with the number of opponents faced. Also has short-range teleportation.
Comments: Jumpings are surprisingly rare in this series, but since he's in LA I have no doubt he's using his power to its fullest off-screen.

Power: Can move at supersonic speeds with the cost of limited effect on the world.
Comments: Kinda disappointing that he's just not that mobile all things considered. Yes, he's a fast runner, but that just doesn't really hold up to those who can fly as fast or faster, or straight-up teleport.

Power: Can fill empty space with razor-sharp forcefields
Comments: Discount Narwhal. He doesn't even really do anything during the Teeth fight, which is weird because his power seems pretty effective.

Power: Steals skills of those nearby, the breadth and depth of the experience taken scaling with the connection formed. Victims will not regain the skill if it is fully stolen.
Comments: The fact that the skills he takes are both general and permanent makes him so much better than Uber it's insane. He still suffers though in that whatever he does is within the human limit in a series where people routinely go beyond that, but this is still one of the best "jack of all trades" powers out there.

Power: Can manipulate space, changing distances and distorting non-living material. Gains more range and control the more time and less people are in an area.
Comments: I really like Vista, but she really should be at home fangirling about Justin Beiber or something. Her power is fantastic though, as spatial manipulation tends to be.

Power: Can temporarily turn into a telekinetic whirlwind.
Comments: I hadn't realized it, but the best feature of this power is that it turns Wanton almost completely invincible for 12+ minutes. The other stuff about the whirlwind is just the cherry on top at that point.

Power: Intense, short-ranged clairvoyance, short bursts of super speed, and the ability to reach into people's bodies and damage internal structures.
Comments: This is such a cool and terrifying mix of powers. I can't see it being used for much but murder, but when he murders, I'm sure Watch murders good.

Power: Case 53, is made of metal, which he bonds to and absorbs upon touch. Can shape, rearrange, and melt the metal composing his body. For the purposes of the Manton Effect, tends to be seen as an object for powers that affect living things, and as a living thing for powers that affect objects.
Comments: Theoretically, he has a fun synergy with Vista where she can cast Enlarge/Reduce on him because of his Manton thing. In fact, the more I think about that aspect of his powers the more interesting he gets.

Power: Telekinesis, but only in a counter-clockwise direction around herself. Effectively a point-blank telekinetic cyclone.
Comments: Wanton but way worse. I don't even think she did anything in the story.

Power: Can dampen physical and mental energy within an area. This causes physical objects to lose kinetic energy, heat and energy attacks to seep away, and people to lose their will to do anything. Can affect a wide range or concentrate power into a smaller but more intense area of effect.
Comments: This is a cool and creative power. It may have been stronger than what was shown in the story since we only saw Winter clones in action and not the original.

Power: Can fly and turn himself into a living projectile that shoots in a straight line.
Comments: The last cape alphabetically, which means he's the last one I'm ranking! Maybe it's because I'm Texan myself, but this whole team kind of stuck in my memory, and I remember thinking Young Buck was really cool. His power is not bad at all, and we know he survived at least two Endbringer-level threats, so respect him!
